label: DocuSign
description: Send, sign, and manage electronic signature envelopes and contracts via DocuSign.

form.title: Connect DocuSign
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to send and manage DocuSign envelopes on your behalf.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Integration Key)
methods.description: Use your DocuSign Integration Key and Secret to authorize Anton via OAuth 2.0. Supports both demo (sandbox) and production environments. Recommended for all users.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 with DocuSign Integration Key

DocuSign calls its OAuth client ID an **Integration Key**. You create one in the DocuSign Admin console.

### Steps

1. Log in to your DocuSign account and open the [DocuSign Admin console](https://admindemo.docusign.com) (demo) or [production Admin](https://admin.docusign.com).
2. In the left sidebar go to **Apps and Keys** (under Integrations).
3. Click **Add App and Integration Key**. Give it a name like `Anton`.
4. Under **Authentication**, select **Authorization Code Grant**.
5. Click **Add Secret Key** and copy the generated secret — it is shown only once.
6. Under **Redirect URIs**, click **Add URI** and enter `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton's local callback).
7. Copy the **Integration Key** (the long UUID at the top of the app page).
8. Paste the Integration Key and Secret into the fields below.
9. Choose your environment — **Production** for live accounts, **Demo / Sandbox** for developer testing.
10. Click **Submit**. Anton will open a browser tab to DocuSign's consent screen; log in and approve access.

### Notes

- Demo and production are completely separate environments with different hostnames (`account-d.docusign.com` vs `account.docusign.com`). Make sure your Integration Key was created in the same environment you select.
- The `extended` scope grants offline access (refresh tokens) so Anton can act without repeated logins.
- To revoke access later, return to **Apps and Keys**, open the app, and delete the secret key or revoke the app entirely.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Integration Key (Client ID)
fields.description: Found in DocuSign Admin → Apps and Keys. It is a UUID that identifies your app.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Generated under the Integration Key's Authentication section. Shown only once — copy it immediately.

fields.name: environment
fields.label: Environment
fields.description: Match this to the DocuSign account where you created the Integration Key.

methods.label: JWT Service Integration
methods.description: Server-to-server authentication using DocuSign's JWT Grant flow. Suitable for automated or headless workflows where no user interaction is possible.
methods.how_to:
## JWT Service Integration (Server-to-Server)

The JWT Grant flow lets Anton act on behalf of a DocuSign user without an interactive browser login. Use this for fully automated pipelines.

### Prerequisites

- A DocuSign Integration Key with **JSON Web Token (JWT)** authentication enabled.
- The user whose account Anton will act as must grant consent at least once.

### Steps

1. Log in to [DocuSign Admin](https://admin.docusign.com) → **Apps and Keys**.
2. Click **Add App and Integration Key**, name it (e.g., `Anton-JWT`).
3. Under **Authentication**, select **JSON Web Token (JWT)**.
4. Click **Generate RSA** — DocuSign generates an RSA key pair. Download the **private key** (PEM format) immediately; it is not stored by DocuSign.
5. Copy the **Integration Key** UUID.
6. Find the **Account ID** (also called API Account ID) on the same Apps and Keys page or under your account settings.
7. Find the **User ID** (Impersonate User ID) under **Admin → Users** for the user Anton will act as.
8. Paste the private key PEM, Integration Key, Account ID, and User ID into the fields below.
9. On first use, DocuSign requires the impersonated user to grant consent once. Anton will prompt you with a consent URL if needed.

### Notes

- The private key PEM is sensitive — treat it like a password.
- Choose the correct environment; demo and production keys are not interchangeable.

fields.name: integration_key
fields.label: Integration Key
fields.description: The UUID from DocuSign Admin → Apps and Keys for your JWT-enabled app.

fields.name: account_id
fields.label: Account ID
fields.description: Your DocuSign API Account ID, found on the Apps and Keys page or under account settings.

fields.name: user_id
fields.label: Impersonate User ID
fields.description: The DocuSign User ID of the account Anton will act as. Found under Admin → Users.

fields.name: private_key_pem
fields.label: RSA Private Key (PEM)
fields.description: Paste the full RSA private key PEM generated in DocuSign Admin. Begins with -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----.

fields.name: environment
fields.label: Environment
fields.description: Must match the environment where your Integration Key was created.
